streamId.description: Stream identifier for event routing (typically the feature/workflow ID).

sequence.description: Monotonically increasing sequence number within the stream (int32, not int64).

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp when the event occurred.

type.description: Event type discriminator (dot-separated, lowercase). Unknown values are logged, not rejected.

correlationId.description: Correlation ID linking related events across workflows.

agentId.description: Agent identifier that produced this event.

source.description: Source system that emitted this event.

data.description: Event-specific data payload — open object so unknown event shapes round-trip.

description:
Base envelope for all SDLC workflow events.

Both Exarchos (JSONL files) and Basileus (Marten streams) use this envelope.
The `type` field is a free-form dot-separated discriminator and the `data`
payload is an open object: an unknown `type` is **representable and logged,
never rejected** (forward compatibility — a consumer that does not recognise
a type retains the raw envelope rather than dropping the event).
